symbols: jeweled Gospel book — the Gospel of Matthew, the divinely inspired text
gold background — the eternal, heavenly realm
Greek inscription — identifying the saint in the liturgical tradition
contemplative gaze — the evangelist pondering the mystery he records
description: An Orthodox icon on a gold background showing a half-length portrait of a bearded man with dark hair and a long beard. He wears a brown inner garment and a dark green outer mantle. He holds an ornate jeweled book with a decorated red and gold cover, partially open to reveal text. Greek letters identifying the saint appear at the upper corners. The figure's expression is thoughtful and solemn. The icon is painted in the traditional Byzantine style with gold leaf background.
theological_description: Saint Matthew, the former tax collector called by Christ from his booth (Matthew 9:9), became the author of the first Gospel. His icon depicts him holding the Gospel he composed — the text that opens the New Testament and bridges the Old and New Covenants through its genealogy tracing Jesus from Abraham and David. Matthew's Gospel was written primarily for a Jewish audience, demonstrating that Jesus fulfills the Law and the Prophets. The jeweled cover of the book signifies the precious value of divine revelation. In Orthodox theology, the Gospel book is treated with the same reverence as the Eucharistic elements — it is enthroned on the altar, processed in the Little Entrance, and kissed by the faithful, because it contains the living Word of God.

scripture_refs.reason: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham — the opening of Matthew's Gospel
Jesus calling Matthew from the tax booth — the evangelist's own conversion story
The Great Commission — Matthew's Gospel culminating in the command to make disciples of all nations
All Scripture is God-breathed — the divine inspiration behind the Gospel Matthew holds
